As OWI Marketing Director, here is my honest answer - the kind I would give my CDO before a roadmap review.

---

## My 6-8 Moments of Value with OWIsMind

**1. Monday Morning Revenue Digest - auto-generated, in my inbox at 8am**

Every Monday I spend 45 minutes pulling numbers from three dashboards, pasting into a slide, writing 4 sentences of commentary, and forwarding to my VP and the CCO. I want OWIsMind to do that at 07:55. Segment-level actuals vs budget vs prior year. Top 5 accounts by variance. One paragraph of plain-language diagnosis. PDF attached, already formatted in Orange brand style. I just hit "forward."

**2. "Explain This Drop" - instant root-cause on any metric dip**

Our Roaming revenue dropped 8% last month. I spent two days asking the data team to slice it by region, by product, by segment, by period. With OWIsMind I type: "Roaming revenue fell 8% in May vs April - break down by product line and customer tier, tell me what's driving it." I get a structured answer with the actual numbers in 30 seconds. This alone replaces a standard analyst request.

**3. Campaign/Segment Performance Brief before QBRs**

Before every quarterly business review I need a one-page brief per key segment: Enterprise, SME, Roaming, Wholesale. Revenue trend (actuals vs budget, 3-month rolling), top accounts, risk accounts (declining trend), opportunity accounts (outpacing budget). Today that is 4 manual files. I want to type "generate the Enterprise QBR brief" and get a PDF I can walk into the room with - or hand directly to the account manager.

**4. Pipeline Influence Check - did our campaigns move revenue?**

Marketing is always asked to prove influence on revenue. I need to correlate campaign activity periods with revenue movement in the relevant segment. Today: impossible without a data analyst and a 2-week wait. With OWIsMind: "show me Roaming revenue trend before, during, and after the Q2 Roaming Hub campaign" - get a chart + the numbers + a plain sentence I can quote to leadership. That one feature justifies the tool to my CMO.

**5. Top Account Digest - "who do I call this week?"**

Every week I want a ranked list: accounts with the biggest positive/negative variance vs budget this week. No commentary needed. Just a clean table I can send to the key account team in 2 clicks. This replaces a manual Monday data pull that currently takes 30 minutes and is always late.

**6. Executive Flash Report - one slide, one number, one sentence**

My CEO gets one number per week: total actuals vs plan, YTD. She does not want a dashboard. She wants one formatted PDF, one sentence of context, delivered Friday afternoon. I want to configure this once and never touch it again.

**7. "What changed since last month?" - conversational YoY/MoM comparison**

I need to be able to ask a follow-up question after seeing a number: "is this better or worse than the same month last year?" or "which segment is most off-plan right now?" in natural language, in the same conversation, without starting over. Conversational continuity is not a nice-to-have - it is what makes this different from a static dashboard.

**8. Shareable Briefing - one-click "send this to my team"**

After I get an answer I like, I need to share it. Not copy-paste into an email. A formatted PDF or a clean HTML snippet I can drop into Teams or Outlook in one action. Today the answer lives only in the chat window and disappears.

---

## Scheduled/Automated Deliverables That Replace Manual Work

- Monday 08:00 weekly digest (PDF) - replaces 45 min manual work weekly
- Friday 17:00 CEO flash (PDF, 1 page) - replaces ad-hoc data team request
- QBR prep brief per segment, triggered D-3 before each QBR date - replaces 4 manual files per quarter
- Monthly YTD summary emailed to the marketing team on the 1st - replaces monthly reporting ritual

---

## 3 Must-Have Features

**1. Scheduled PDF digest with email delivery.** Not a link to a dashboard. A real PDF, formatted, in my inbox, on schedule. This is the feature that gets adopted across the whole commercial team without training.

**2. Downloadable chart PNG / table CSV from any answer.** I need to drop a chart into a PowerPoint in 10 seconds. The PNG must look like a real deliverable, not a screenshot. This is the feature that makes OWIsMind a source of truth in presentations.

**3. Verified numbers - with visible grounding.** Every number I put in front of an executive must be traceable. I need to see "this €4.2M actuals figure comes from the ACTUALS scenario, YTD May 2026, Segment: Enterprise, Source: Drive Revenues dataset, last refreshed 2026-06-18." One wrong number in a board meeting and I lose credibility permanently. I will not use a tool that does not show me its work.

---

## Trust and Presentation-Quality Requirements

Zero tolerance for unverified numbers. Every figure must cite its source, scenario, and period filter - visible in the UI and printed in the PDF. If the system is uncertain, it says so explicitly; it does not invent a confident-sounding answer.

Formatting must be executive-ready out of the box: consistent fonts, no chart-junk, branded. I cannot spend time cleaning up outputs before a board presentation.

One hallucinated revenue number ends adoption. Business users will not give a second chance. The grounding and verification layer is not a feature - it is the price of entry.
